assertions.text: Research steps are specific to the content type
Steps ordered from fastest to slowest

content-type-specific.note: Steps include defunct newspaper research, Companies House search, photographer registries (DACS)

ordered-fast-to-slow.note: Starts with metadata check, then publisher research, then registries, then formal enquiries

orphan-works.note: UK orphan works licensing scheme described with IPO reference

content-type-specific.note: Steps include YouTube metadata extraction, reverse image search, and content ID research

ordered-fast-to-slow.note: Quick digital checks first, then platform enquiry, then broader research

orphan-works.note: Orphan works addressed with note about fair use considerations

content-type-specific.note: Steps include SIAE (Italian collecting society), RAI archive department, and orchestra successor research

ordered-fast-to-slow.note: RAI archive search first, then SIAE, then broader Italian registry search

orphan-works.note: Italian orphan works provisions and EU directive referenced
